pi_items.source: setup_task.sh and neo_obs_request.txt explicitly name the target as 2020 QG.
setup_task.sh injects RA '16h 52m 58s', which converts to exactly 16.88277... hours, matching the 16.883 claim. verifier.py also hardcodes TARGET_RA = 16.883.
setup_task.sh injects Dec '-21d 55m 00s', which converts to exactly -21.91666... degrees, matching the -21.917 claim. verifier.py hardcodes TARGET_DEC = -21.917.
setup_task.sh observing requirements note states 'accuracy within 2 degrees'. verifier.py enforces COORD_TOL_DEG = 2.0.
setup_task.sh observing requirements state 'Minimum number of frames: 6'. verifier.py requires valid_count >= 6 for full score.
setup_task.sh observing requirements state 'Exposure time: 30 seconds per frame'.
setup_task.sh observing requirements state CCD upload directory: /home/ga/Images/asteroids/2020QG/. verifier.py checks this specific directory via export_result.sh.
setup_task.sh observing requirements state astrometry report to /home/ga/Documents/mpc_report.txt. export_result.sh explicitly checks this path.
setup_task.sh writes the request document directly to /home/ga/Documents/neo_obs_request.txt.
General context match, standard occupational classification.
Task context exactly involves Near-Earth Object Observation and planetary defense.

privileged_info_summary: The task operates in a simulated environment (INDI CCD/Telescope Simulator) to observe the near-Earth asteroid 2020 QG. The required target coordinates are right ascension 16.883 hours (16h 52m 58s) and declination -21.917 degrees (-21d 55m 00s), with a 2.0-degree tolerance. The agent must capture a minimum of 6 FITS images with 30-second exposures saved to /home/ga/Images/asteroids/2020QG, and output an astrometry report to /home/ga/Documents/mpc_report.txt.
